meaning: adj.易坏的,脆弱的,浅薄的,没有价值的,不足信的,(人)浮夸的n.薄纸,描图用薄纸,薄纸稿纸
examples: The evidence against him is pretty flimsy.
The charges were based on very flimsy evidence.
She was wearing only a thin robe over a flimsy nightgown, and her feet were bare.
But AZT has a structural weakness (a flimsy phosphate) that viral enzymes can expel.
Strategery represented a flimsy veneer of forethought over a largely reactive and often ineffective game-plan.
